item.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

item.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

item.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

item.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

item.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

item.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

item.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

item.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

item.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

item.title: Tokenmaxxing, OpenAI’s shopping spree, and the AI Anxiety Gap
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha, Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane
item.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps to talk shows, a certain shoe company just rebranded as an AI infrastructure play, and Anthropic unveiled a model it says is too powerful to release publicly …but apparently not too […]

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.

item.title: Are we tokenmaxxing our way to nowhere?
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The gap between AI insiders and everyone else is widening, and the spending, suspicion, and even new vocabulary are starting to show it. While OpenAI is busy buying up everything from finance apps to talk shows, a certain shoe company just rebranded as an AI infrastructure play, and Anthropic unveiled a model it says is too powerful to release publicly …but apparently not too […]

item.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

item.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

item.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

item.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

item.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI's agentic coding tool has gotten a major makeover, with a variety of new powers and abilities.

item.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Krieger's departure and any forthcoming design tools will be another data point for investors who fear the SaaSpocalypse — that the largest AI labs will come to dominate software businesses, a thesis that has rocked public markets at times this year.

item.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with AI Mode.
